title: US–China Chip Supply Chain Lens
scope: A stage-based semiconductor observatory that shows where connector economies sit and move between United States and China trade exposure while keeping customs observations, industrial context, policy events, and analytical estimates separate.

perspective.principle: Neutral reported observations, explicit United States–China analytical lens, and no hidden geopolitical score.
questions: Is the selected economy or stage currently more exposed to the United States or China within the two-anchor sample?
Is that position moving toward the United States, toward China, or remaining stable?
Which stages and products contribute to the observed position?
Is exposure concentrated on one anchor or material on both anchors?
Which dated official policy and project events provide context without claiming causality?

data_policy.rule: TradeGravity may use openly accessible official, intergovernmental, or public-web evidence and must keep source terms and measurement limits visible.
exclusions: paywalled evidence required to reproduce a published metric

open_datasets.title: UN Comtrade API
open_datasets.granularity: reporter × partner × flow × HS6 × annual/monthly period
open_datasets.role: Observed USA/China trade position, annual direction, focused monthly turning points and mirror diagnostics
open_datasets.reuse_note: Public API; retain source classification, period, partner, flow and UN Comtrade attribution.

open_datasets.title: WITS and UNCTAD TRAINS
open_datasets.granularity: bilateral totals and tariff schedule by reporter/importer, partner, product and period
open_datasets.role: Headline history and reproducible tariff context
open_datasets.reuse_note: Public web/API access; retain provider, nomenclature, tariff data type and reuse terms.

open_datasets.title: World Bank Open Data API
open_datasets.reuse_note: Public API under World Bank data terms; indicators are context and not semiconductor capacity.

open_datasets.title: 2025 edition Inter-Country Input-Output tables
open_datasets.granularity: country × economic activity × origin/destination × year through 2022
open_datasets.role: Lagged industry-level value-added and propagation context; never substituted for semiconductor HS6 observations
open_datasets.reuse_note: Public download with OECD attribution; industry aggregation and release lag must remain explicit.

open_datasets.title: Dated policy, award and project pages
open_datasets.role: Policy and project context kept separate from customs values and operating capacity
open_datasets.reuse_note: Public official pages; quote sparingly, link the dated source, and preserve announced/planned/operating status.

caveats: HS6 trade does not identify fab ownership, process node, wafer capacity, HBM generation, advanced-packaging technology, design IP, EDA services, or physical shipment routes.
Broad proxy codes can include non-semiconductor end uses. Every stage declares whether a code is direct, legacy, or a broad proxy.
Country roles are curated context from official and intergovernmental sources, not measured market shares or investment advice.
Announced capacity and operating capacity must never be combined; this reference release does not publish proprietary fab-capacity estimates.

stages.label: Design, IP & EDA
stages.description: Architecture, chip design, reusable IP and electronic-design-automation software.
stages.gap: Mostly intangible services and intellectual property, so HS goods trade cannot observe this stage directly.

stages.label: Materials & wafers
stages.description: High-purity silicon, doped wafers and broad lithography-chemical proxies.
stages.gap: Purity, semiconductor grade and final end use are not consistently visible in HS6.

stages.label: Manufacturing equipment
stages.description: Wafer, device, assembly, inspection and related equipment and parts.
stages.gap: HS6 does not identify tool model, supported node, installed base or end-user fab.

stages.label: Logic & foundry output
stages.description: Processors, controllers, amplifiers and other integrated circuits used as a customs-trade proxy for fabricated logic output.
stages.gap: Foundry ownership, fabless origin and process node cannot be inferred from the exported integrated circuit.

stages.label: Memory & HBM
stages.description: Memory integrated circuits, with HBM treated as current industry context rather than a separately observed HS6 product.
stages.gap: HS6 854232 combines memory types and cannot separate DRAM, NAND, HBM generation or package configuration.

stages.label: Discrete, power & sensors
stages.description: Diodes, transistors, power switches, photosensitive devices and semiconductor transducers.
stages.gap: The HS 2022 revision split several legacy categories; comparisons require revision-aware code selection.

stages.label: Packaging, substrates & test
stages.description: Assembly machinery, printed circuits, IC parts, inspection and test-equipment proxies.
stages.gap: OSAT activity, 2.5D/3D packaging, chiplets and substrate technology are not uniquely identified by HS6.

stages.label: Downstream compute & network demand
stages.description: Broad processing units and network equipment used only as downstream demand context.
stages.gap: These products are not semiconductor output and cannot isolate AI servers or semiconductor content.

country_roles.name: United States
country_roles.note: Strong design, EDA, equipment and AI-compute demand context; domestic manufacturing and packaging policy is expanding.

country_roles.name: Chinese Taipei
country_roles.note: Foundry and advanced-packaging specialization is material to leading-edge supply-chain monitoring.

country_roles.name: Korea, Rep.
country_roles.note: Memory and HBM are central roles, alongside foundry and advanced-packaging investment.

country_roles.name: Japan
country_roles.note: Materials, wafers and manufacturing equipment are prominent supply-chain roles; next-generation fabrication support is expanding.

country_roles.name: Netherlands
country_roles.note: Lithography equipment makes this an important equipment-stage context node.

country_roles.name: China
country_roles.note: Large manufacturing, assembly and end-demand context with rapidly changing export-control exposure.

country_roles.name: Malaysia
country_roles.note: Assembly, packaging and test specialization makes the country relevant to diversification analysis.

country_roles.name: Singapore
country_roles.note: Regional manufacturing, equipment and assembly hub context.

country_roles.name: Philippines
country_roles.note: Assembly and test specialization is relevant to Southeast Asian supply-chain mapping.

country_roles.name: Viet Nam
country_roles.note: Growing electronics assembly and packaging context; customs data should not be treated as proof of physical rerouting.

trends.label: AI shifts the bottleneck
trends.summary: AI demand is increasing the strategic importance of advanced logic, HBM, substrates, power delivery and advanced packaging rather than chips in aggregate.

trends.label: Capacity investment remains elevated
trends.summary: SEMI projects double-digit growth in global 300 mm fab-equipment spending in both 2026 and 2027, driven by AI and regionalization.

trends.label: Policy is now a first-class supply variable
trends.summary: Controls now cover advanced computing, semiconductor equipment, software, HBM and foundry due diligence, while industrial programs support regional capacity.

trends.label: Competition is ecosystem-wide
trends.summary: Current strategies increasingly connect design, manufacturing, packaging, equipment, data, power, talent and security rather than treating a fab as an isolated asset.

policy_events.title: Gallium and germanium-related export controls announced
European Chips Act enters into force
2024 integrated-circuit and software tax-incentive eligibility notice
Controls expanded to equipment, software and HBM
Advanced-computing controls and foundry due diligence strengthened
Selected medium and heavy rare-earth export controls issued
VEU loophole for foreign-owned fabs in China closed
Rapidus selected for next-generation semiconductor support
License review policy revised for selected advanced accelerators to China
Technology-sovereignty package outlines Chips Act 2.0 direction
Semiconductor and digital-industry strategy integrates AI, compute, power and security

capacity_signals.title: TSMC Arizona final CHIPS incentives award
capacity_signals.claim: NIST states that the award supports more than $65 billion in planned investment across three leading-edge fabs. TradeGravity records the award and plan without treating all three fabs as operating capacity.

capacity_signals.title: SK hynix advanced-packaging and R&D facility in Indiana
capacity_signals.claim: The official CHIPS project page describes a high-volume advanced-packaging line for next-generation HBM with production expected in the second half of 2028.

capacity_signals.title: Eighteen new semiconductor fabs expected to start construction in 2025
capacity_signals.claim: SEMI forecast 18 new fab construction starts and projected industry capacity growth; it is an aggregate forecast, not operating-capacity evidence.

capacity_signals.title: Rapidus next-generation semiconductor production plan selected for support
capacity_signals.claim: METI selected the plan under Japan's next-generation semiconductor support framework; TradeGravity does not convert the announcement into operating capacity.

capacity_signals.title: First four EU IPF/OEF semiconductor project designations
capacity_signals.claim: The European Commission granted IPF or OEF status to four projects in Germany, Austria and Italy. The register retains the designation and stated project targets rather than aggregating them as current output.

capacity_signals.title: Silicon Box chiplet packaging facility receives Open EU Foundry status
capacity_signals.claim: The European Commission describes a panel-level advanced packaging and testing facility and states that full capacity is expected in 2033; this is not counted as current production.

capacity_signals.title: Global 300 mm fab-equipment spending forecast to rise in 2026 and 2027
capacity_signals.claim: SEMI projects double-digit equipment-spending growth driven by AI and regionalization; spending is not the same as installed or productive capacity.

capacity_signals.title: 300 mm memory-equipment investment forecast above $50 billion in 2026
capacity_signals.claim: SEMI links the investment outlook to HBM and DDR5 demand; the forecast does not identify HBM generation or country-level output.

scenario_defaults.warning: A transparent exposure sensitivity based on observed trade, not a capacity, price, GDP, welfare or causal forecast.

publication.scope: UN Comtrade reporter observations against USA and China partners; not total world semiconductor trade or physical routes
publication.measurement_description: Coverage gates describe published stage-mapped HS6 observations. Passing them does not reveal capacity, process node, firms, services or shipment routes.
